name: Extrahepatic biliary tree anatomy

figure_scopes.overview: Liver, gallbladder, and the extrahepatic ducts down to the duodenum, with the portal triad relationship
figure_scopes.closeup: Duct confluence or portal triad cross-relationship

laterality: Portal triad: portal vein posterior, proper hepatic artery medial/left, bile duct lateral/right
must_have: Right hepatic duct, left hepatic duct, common hepatic duct, cystic duct, and common bile duct.
Proper hepatic artery and portal vein.
spatial_rules: Right and left hepatic ducts join to form the common hepatic duct.
Cystic duct joins the common hepatic duct to form the common bile duct.
Keep the portal vein posterior and the proper hepatic artery medial/left within the portal triad relationship.

forbidden_errors.error: Invented accessory ducts added unless the user explicitly asks for variants.
Portal vein drawn anterior in the portal triad.
Cystic duct shown not joining the common hepatic duct.

safety_concept: Duct confluence: right + left hepatic ducts form the common hepatic duct; adding the cystic duct forms the common bile duct.
verify: Right and left hepatic ducts join to form the common hepatic duct.
Cystic duct joins the common hepatic duct to form the common bile duct.
Portal vein remains posterior in the portal triad relationship.
